title: TradeZella Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of TradeZella pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
TradeZella Pricing Overview

TradeZella is a premium trading journal designed for serious traders who need advanced analytics, trade replay, and backtesting capabilities. The platform takes a straightforward approach to pricing with just two paid tiers and no free tier option. Starting at $29 per month for the Basic plan, TradeZella offers a feature-rich solution across stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto markets.

Unlike many competitors that use freemium models to capture casual traders, TradeZella has positioned itself as a professional-grade tool from the start. Founded in 2021 and headquartered in Miami, Florida, the company focuses on depth over breadth—building features that matter most to day traders, prop firm traders, and swing traders who rely on detailed performance analytics and trade reviews to improve their edge.

If you're looking for a zero-cost option, TradeZella isn't it. But if you're serious about tracking and learning from your trades, the investment starts at less than $1 per day with the Basic plan.

All TradeZella Plans Compared

TradeZella offers two pricing tiers. Here's what you get at each level:

Feature Basic Pro
Monthly Price $29/mo $49/mo
Connected Accounts 1 Account Up to 20 Accounts
Trade Journaling ✓ ✓
Trade Replay ✓ ✓
Broker Integration ✓ ✓
Performance Analytics ✓ ✓
Backtesting ✓ ✓
Risk Management Tools ✓ ✓
Multi-Account Dashboards — ✓

Basic Plan ($29/mo): The entry point into TradeZella's ecosystem. You get core journaling, trade replay, automatic broker imports, and performance analytics on a single connected account. All the essential features for analyzing your trading decisions are here. The limitation is obvious: you can only track one broker account at a time. This works fine for traders focusing on a single market or account.

Pro Plan ($49/mo): For traders managing multiple accounts—perhaps separate accounts for different brokers, strategies, or account types—the Pro plan ($49/month) unlocks up to 20 connected accounts and consolidated dashboards. The $20 monthly difference ($240 annually) adds significant value if you're juggling accounts across multiple brokers. Most serious traders will eventually upgrade here.

Note: TradeZella's tool data does not specify annual billing discounts. Many competitors offer 15-20% savings for annual prepayment, but TradeZella's pricing page may or may not include such options. We recommend checking their site directly for current annual pricing availability.

Free Plan / Free Trial

TradeZella does not offer a free tier or free trial.

This is a significant departure from competitors like Edgewonk and TradingView, which offer limited free plans or extended trial periods. If you want to evaluate TradeZella before committing, you'll need to pay for the Basic plan ($29/mo) to get started. Some traders view this as a positive—it filters for committed users and signals that the company isn't trying to monetize user data or ads. Others see it as a barrier.

If budget is tight, the $29 Basic plan is the lowest entry cost, and you can cancel anytime if it doesn't fit your workflow. But there's no "test drive" option, so research reviews carefully before committing.

Hidden Costs and Fees

TradeZella's pricing is transparent on the surface, but traders should watch for these potential add-on costs:

  • Market Data Feeds: Depending on your broker, real-time market data may not be included. Some brokers require separate data subscriptions for options and futures. TradeZella imports what your broker provides, but if your broker charges for advanced data, you'll pay that separately.
  • Broker Integration Fees: TradeZella integrates with most major brokers (Interactive Brokers, Thinkorswim, Alpaca, Crypto.com, etc.), but some brokers charge fees for API access or data export. Check with your broker before switching.
  • Payment Processing: Credit card payments may include standard payment processor fees, though TradeZella's site should indicate if they absorb these costs.
  • No Hidden Overages: TradeZella doesn't appear to charge per trade logged, per account connection, or for storage. The plans are all-you-can-trade within the account limits.

The bottom line: The $29–$49 monthly cost is your main expense. Add-ons are minimal compared to competitors.

TradeZella Pricing vs Competitors

How does TradeZella stack up against other trading journals?

Platform Basic/Entry Price Free Tier Account Limit (Entry)
TradeZella $29/mo No 1 Account
Edgewonk Free (limited); $25/mo (Pro) Yes, Limited 1 Account (Free)
TradingView Free; $12.95/mo (Premium) Yes Unlimited (All tiers)
Thinkorswim (TD Ameritrade) Free Yes Unlimited

vs. Edgewonk ($25–40/mo): Edgewonk's Pro plan is slightly cheaper at $25/month but only includes one account. To connect multiple accounts, you'll upgrade to the $40/month tier. TradeZella's Pro at $49/month gives you 20 accounts, making it more cost-effective for multi-account traders. Edgewonk has a free tier, though it's limited to 10 trades per month—TradeZella doesn't.

vs. TradingView ($12.95–199/mo): TradingView's premium plans start lower at $12.95/month, but that's primarily a charting and analysis platform with journaling as a secondary feature. TradeZella is purpose-built for journaling and backtesting. TradingView's core value isn't journaling; it's charts. For pure journaling, TradeZella is more feature-focused.

vs. Thinkorswim (Free): If you trade with TD Ameritrade, Thinkorswim's built-in paper money and performance tracker are free. However, Thinkorswim's journal features are basic compared to TradeZella's trade replay, advanced analytics, and backtesting. Thinkorswim is a broker's platform; TradeZella is a specialist journal tool.

Verdict: TradeZella is priced higher than some competitors but offers depth in journaling and backtesting. If you need a no-cost option, look at Edgewonk's free tier or TradingView. If you need professional-grade replay and multi-account tracking, TradeZella justifies the premium.

Is TradeZella Worth the Price?

For Day Traders and Prop Firm Traders: Absolutely. The trade replay feature is excellent for visual learning, and the automatic broker imports eliminate manual logging. At $29/month ($348/year), you're paying less than most trading education courses, and this tool directly improves your execution. If you trade enough to generate $100+ in daily losses or gains, this tool pays for itself through one better decision per month.

For Swing Traders: Worth it if you trade multiple accounts or strategies. A single-account swing trader might find the Basic plan sufficient and cancel in slower months. Multi-strategy swing traders should jump to Pro ($49/mo) for the multi-account capability.

For Crypto Traders: TradeZella supports crypto markets, making it a solid choice for traders who trade both stocks and digital assets. The $29–49 price is competitive in a niche where many platforms are either free-but-basic or $100+/month.

For Part-Time Traders: The ROI calculation changes. If you trade 2–3 times per week, the $29/month may feel high until you realize even one avoided mistake per month covers the cost. Still, budget-conscious part-time traders should evaluate the free tiers offered by competitors first.

Account Upgrade Trigger: Upgrade from Basic to Pro when you manage 2+ broker accounts or run multiple strategies on the same broker. The consolidated dashboard becomes valuable, and $20/month is negligible if you're serious enough to be scaling trading complexity.

How to Save on TradeZella

Annual Billing Discount: Check TradeZella's pricing page for annual prepayment options. Many SaaS tools offer 10–20% off when you pay upfront. If available, annual billing saves $35–118/year.

Strategic Timing: If TradeZella occasionally runs promotional campaigns (common around trading conferences or year-end), signing up during promotions could unlock a first-month discount or extended trial.

Broker Partnerships: Some brokers bundle or discount third-party tools for their customers. Check if your broker offers a TradeZella discount as part of a partnership program.

Contest or Referral Bonuses: Trading communities sometimes run contests or referral programs where you can earn credits. Monitor TradeZella's social media or trading forums for opportunities.

Honest Take: TradeZella doesn't advertise student discounts or non-profit pricing. The platform prices itself as a professional tool, not a budget tool. If cost is a dealbreaker, consider Edgewonk's free tier first, then upgrade to TradeZella once your trading income justifies the investment.

Bottom Line: At $29–49/month, TradeZella is a reasonable cost for a tool that can improve your trading discipline and decision-making. The lack of a free tier is intentional—it attracts committed traders and keeps the user base quality-focused. For serious traders, it's an investment worth making. For hobbyists testing the waters, explore free alternatives first.

Ready to see more details? Check out our full TradeZella review or compare all trading journals on TradingToolsHub.
